positioning.statement: For boards and executives navigating complex stakeholder environments, Duke Strategies is the strategic advisory firm that makes business strategies stakeholder-proof, because we combine corporate strategy depth with communications craft, AI-driven intelligence, and direct government and security expertise.
positioning.differentiator: makes business strategies stakeholder-proof by integrating strategy, communications, and AI-driven stakeholder intelligence

coreMessage.full: Business strategies succeed or fail based on stakeholder acceptance — we make them stakeholder-proof before they face scrutiny.

elevatorPitch.text: Duke Strategies helps boards and executives make their business strategies stakeholder-proof. We operate at the intersection of corporate strategy and corporate affairs, using AI-driven stakeholder intelligence and decades of crisis, M&A, and government relations experience. In our first three months, we helped around 15 companies make clear strategic choices.

alternatives: Where corporate strategy meets corporate affairs
Building bridges between business leaders and stakeholders

messageLengths.headline: We make business strategies stakeholder-proof
messageLengths.short: Duke Strategies is a strategic advisory consultancy that operates at the intersection of corporate strategy and corporate affairs. Founded by former Hill+Knowlton Strategies leaders Ingo Heijnen and Arien Stuijt, we help boards make their strategies stakeholder-proof through AI-driven stakeholder intelligence, crisis-tested advisory, and direct political and security expertise.
messageLengths.full: Duke Strategies is a strategic advisory consultancy that operates at the intersection of corporate strategy and corporate affairs, helping business leaders build bridges with their stakeholders. Founded in 2025 by former Hill+Knowlton Strategies leaders Ingo Heijnen (former CEO H&K Netherlands and Continental Europe) and Arien Stuijt (former Strategy Director and Wall Street Journal journalist), we combine four decades of communications leadership with AI-driven stakeholder intelligence to make business strategies stakeholder-proof. Our Expert Partners include a former Minister of Economic Affairs and a former Director of Military Intelligence, giving clients direct access to government and security insight. We use a three-step advisory model — Analyse, Create, Execute — that reveals stakeholder blind spots, develops a Single Most Important Action, and supports impactful implementation. In our first three months, we helped around 15 companies make clear strategic choices.

voice.tone: Bold and direct — we challenge assumptions, tell it like it is, and make a choice with our clients rather than presenting a set of options. Anti-jargon. Confident but grounded in evidence, not self-promotion.

strategicNarrative.oldWorld: Business strategies were traditionally developed on financial logic and market analysis alone. Communications was an afterthought — brought in to 'sell' the strategy to stakeholders after decisions were made. Stakeholder engagement meant press releases and annual reports.
strategicNarrative.shift: Stakeholders today see through the nonsense. Shareholders, employees, regulators, communities, and the public demand that what executives do is responsible, explainable, and serves broad interests. Strategies that cannot survive stakeholder scrutiny fail — not because the business logic was wrong, but because the stakeholder landscape was ignored. A strategy that can't be communicated is a strategy that won't be executed.
strategicNarrative.newWorld: The companies that succeed are those whose strategies are stakeholder-proof from the start — stress-tested against political, regulatory, media, community, and investor realities before they face scrutiny. These companies integrate strategy and communications as one discipline, use data to map stakeholder dynamics in real time, and make one clear choice rather than hedging across options.
strategicNarrative.yourRole: Duke Strategies was founded to close the gap between corporate strategy and corporate affairs. We don't give clients a set of options and a 185-slide deck — we help them discover the one thing that matters, make a clear choice, and execute it across every stakeholder group. Our analyses reveal blind spots that traditional advisory misses.
strategicNarrative.stakes: Companies that separate strategy from stakeholder reality face escalating costs: crises that could have been prevented, deals that collapse under stakeholder pressure, regulatory battles that drain resources, and reputational damage that compounds. In an era of heightened scrutiny, the cost of stakeholder-blind strategy is not just expensive — it's existential.

primaryAlternatives: Traditional management/strategy consultants (McKinsey, BCG) — focus on numbers alone
Communications agencies — execute messaging without questioning underlying strategy
Big Four advisory — broad but generic, junior-heavy
positioningGaps: Integration of corporate strategy and corporate affairs as one discipline
AI-driven stakeholder intelligence (not just traditional research)
Senior-only engagement model (no junior consultants running your account)
competitiveAdvantages: Stakeholder-proof strategy testing from day one
AI-driven stakeholder mapping and coalition detection
Expert Partners with direct government and intelligence leadership experience
Journalistic rigor (WSJ methodology) applied to strategic intelligence
One clear recommendation, not a set of options

reviewTriggers: New Expert Partner or senior hire
Expansion to new geography or service line
Significant media coverage or thought leadership pivot
